# @oimdb/exodra

> **For AI assistants:** Adapter from OIMDB to [Exodra](https://exodra.org) reactivity. It is the
> Exodra counterpart of `@oimdb/react`: instead of a hook + re-render, every OIMDB source becomes a
> read-only Exodra bindable (`TExoBindable<T> = { getValue(): T; subscribe(update): () => void }`).
> Read `@oimdb/core`'s reference first — this package only adapts its selector layer. Depends only on the type-only `@exodra/reactivity-types` (no reactivity runtime); peer dep `@oimdb/core`.

## Model

- OIMDB `OIMSelector<T>` is `{ getValue(): T, watch(onValue): () => void }`; Exodra `TExoBindable<T>`
  is `{ getValue(): T, subscribe(update): () => void }`. Same shape → this package is a thin adapter.
- Fine-grained subscription (index key for membership + each pk in the set, re-subscribing when the
  set changes) already lives in the `@oimdb/core` selectors. This package does NOT re-implement it.
- All produced bindables are **read-only**, **lazy** (subscribe upstream only while they have a
  downstream subscriber → cost O(visible)) and **SSR-safe** (`getValue()` reads fresh from the store,
  valid without an active subscription).
- OIMDB delivers on `queue.flush()`. Emits are deduped; the initial synchronous emit that OIMDB's
  `watch` fires on subscribe is swallowed (getValue already carries the value).

## Adapter (core)

```ts
fromSelector<T>(selector: OIMSelector<T>, opts?: TOIMExodraBindableOptions<T>): TExoBindable<T>
fromOimdb<T>(read: () => T, subscribe: (onChange: () => void) => () => void, opts?): TExoBindable<T>
fromSelectorFactory<TKey, T>(key: TKey | TOIMExodraReadable<TKey>, makeSelector: (key: TKey) => OIMSelector<T>, opts?): TExoBindable<T>
fromComputed<T>(computed: OIMComputed<T>, opts?): TExoBindable<T>
combine<T>(sources: readonly TOIMExodraReadable<unknown>[], fn: () => T): TExoBindable<T>
```

`TOIMExodraBindableOptions<T> = { equals?: (a: T, b: T) => boolean; alwaysNotify?: boolean }`.
`equals` defaults to `Object.is`. Use `alwaysNotify: true` with in-place entity updaters (stable
entity reference, where `Object.is` would swallow the update). `combine` is the multi-source escape
hatch — Exodra's own `derive` is single-source.

`fromComputed`: read via `computed.get()`, subscribe via `computed.updateEventEmitter.subscribeOnKey('value', …)`.
An `OIMComputed` is one scalar cell; keep any fan-in inside it and forward the final value — do not
chain several Exodra `derive`s (Exodra has no glitch-batching).

## Selector mirror

```ts
bindSelectors<TEntity, TPk>(selectors: OIMCollectionSelectors<TEntity, TPk>, defaults?)
```

Returns an object mirroring `OIMCollectionSelectors`, each method returning a `TExoBindable` and
accepting its key as `TKey | TOIMExodraReadable<TKey>` (a plain value OR a bindable — a bindable key
re-points the subscription on change without recreating the view). The collection's queue is private,
so the entry point is the `OIMCollectionSelectors` instance (which owns a queue-bound compute
runtime), not a bare collection.

Methods: `byPk`, `byPks`, `entitiesBySetIndexKey`, `entitiesByArrayIndexKey`,
`entitiesByCompositeSetIndexKey`, `entitiesByCompositeArrayIndexKey`, `entitiesByArrayGlobalIndex`,
`entitiesBySetGlobalIndex`. Entity readers return `TEntity | undefined`; multi readers return
`readonly (TEntity | undefined)[]` (length-aligned with holes, matching `@oimdb/react`).

## Lists (identity-stable children)

Exodra `list()` is op-based with no key function; identity comes from caching a row schema by a
stable key (a field edit that doesn't change the key set is a reconcile no-op — the row's own inner
bindable updates in place, focus survives).

```ts
keyedChildren<TItem, TSchema>(source: TExoBindable<readonly TItem[]>, opts: { key: (i: TItem) => TOIMKey; render: (i: TItem) => TSchema }): TExoBindable<readonly TSchema[]>
entityRows<TEntity, TPk, TSchema>(order: TExoBindable<readonly TPk[]>, bindEntity: (pk: TPk) => TExoBindable<TEntity | undefined>, render: (entity: TExoBindable<TEntity | undefined>, pk: TPk) => TSchema): TExoBindable<readonly TSchema[]>
```

`entityRows` keys by pk and gives each row its own entity bindable (self-updating, focus-safe),
subscriptions O(visible).

For large ordered lists, the O(delta) path bridges an OIMDB ordered-list command stream (whose
commands are shape-identical to `TExoListOp`) to an Exodra `bindableList`:

```ts
listFromCommandStream<TKey, TItem, TSchema>(stream: IOIMOrderedListCommandSource<TKey, TItem>, key: TKey, render: (item: TItem) => TSchema): TExoBindableList<TSchema>
```

Feed it `createOIMOrderedListCommandStreamDiffDriven(queue, derivedArrayIndex)` (or a writer/
collection stream). Lazy (subscribes the stream only while the list has an ops subscriber);
`snapshot()` reads the current order synchronously (SSR-safe). A diff-driven stream emits `move` (not
remove+insert) on reorder, so the DOM node is relocated; the carried item is an entity slot
(`{ pk, item }`).

## Low-level (find-replace migration)

Mirror a typical inline app bridge 1:1 (same names/signatures) — migrating is only an import-path
change. Also for `onExoMount`-scoped subscriptions (bucket bindings subscribe at *build*, not mount).

```ts
readEntityByPk(collection, pk): TEntity | undefined
subscribeEntityByPk(collection, pk, onChange): () => void
readPksByIndexKey(index, key): TPk[]
subscribePksByIndexKey(index, key, onChange): () => void
readEntitiesByIndexKey(collection, index, key): TEntity[]           // compact (missing dropped)
subscribeEntitiesByIndexKey(collection, index, key, onChange): () => void  // fine-grained, not a firehose
```

Structural input interfaces: `IOIMExodraReadableCollection`, `IOIMExodraSetBasedIndex`
(satisfied by `OIMReactiveCollection` / `OIMReactiveIndexSetBased`).
